signals.reasoning: Close the live ETH long now: it is already back to flat/slightly red after only peaking at +$0.09, retains none of peak P&L, is flagged urgent, has no stop, and the board is under active VETO, so this is not a position worth defending.
BTC still holds constructive trend-supported structure, but the active VETO and lack of an open BTC position make it context only rather than a fresh action during ETH position management.
SOL remains firm and is not breaking down cleanly enough for a new short, while the VETO regime keeps it from being a fresh long chase.
GRIFFAIN is already deep into a long-liquidation flush with very negative funding, so it is too extended to chase short and too damaged to trust long under the event brake.
POLYX has the best raw squeeze fuel on the sheet with deeply negative funding, but it is thin, extended, and still blocked by the live VETO from becoming an actual trade.
CHILLGUY still reads more like a short-covering bounce than a clean new trend, which makes it watch-only rather than actionable under VETO.
XPL is constructive but already extended and still incomplete on the mechanical side, so it stays watch-only while position management focuses on the weak ETH long.
BIO is too small and incomplete mechanically to justify attention over simply flattening the compromised ETH position first.
LDO still has strong upside structure, but the move is already stretched and the event veto keeps it below the bar for a new trade.
MERL has recovered well but still lacks the depth and clarity needed to outrank the immediate ETH cleanup decision.
